entry.title: Re: Credit exposure and credit segment not updated
entry.content:
Hi,

Your specific set-up is not clear, however, on assumption, it could be worth checking for:

1. Any missing assignment of Credit Control Area to the country Credit Segment (Table T014).

2. If the country specific Segment has flag for adding contribution to Main Segment (Table UKMCRED_SGM0C - MAIN_CREDSGM_ADD).

3. If the country specific Segment has a credit check flag for only the Main Segment, rather than a Credit check on individual Segment level (Table UKMCRED_SGM0C - FLEXIBLE_CHECK).

Best.

Georgios

entry.title: DE_CGI_XML_CT_V9: Building number appended to street name
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

Using a copy of DE_CGI_XML_CT_V9, I noticed that the building number (N_2528565240 - BldgNb) is not populated.

The building number node appears to be empty. It has an empty constant:

Instead, the building number appears to be appended to the street name node (N_0177746670 - StrNm) using the FPAYHX-REF09+070(60) field:

I did some tinkering and filled the <BldgNb> with FPAYHX-REF08+060(10).
But now I still have the duplicate building numbers in <StrNm> and <BldgNb> :

I believe this issue can be addressed for private cloud systems in the following note: https://me.sap.com/notes/3446890

  • All CT Formats - <CdtTrfTxInf><Cdtr><PstlAdr><BldgNb> is filled separately (instead of concatenated value in <StrtNm>)

Is there anything I can do to fix this, or will I have to hope that 2408 fixes this?

Thanks

Simon

entry.title: Re: Active Directory and SAP S/4 integration via BTP
entry.content:
Hi 

The scenario you're describing is part of the scope of an IDM Solution. 

SAP is deprecating its SAP IDM solution and migrating to Microsoft (https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/microsoft-entra-blog/sap-identity-management-to-microsoft-entra-id-migration-guidance/ba-p/2520428)

you'll see some provisioning scenarios like your'e mentioning here:

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/scenarios/migrate-from-sap-idm#provision-to-sap-systems

You can also work with Cloud Identity Services and provision via IPS 

https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/microsoft-active-directory

I'd suggest to go for EntraID as a Source since you'll get more features and possibilities in general.

The scenarios you're mentioning are probably feasible but will require implementation effort.

entry.title: Vendor Rebates - SAP S4HANA
entry.content:
A vendor rebate is a financial incentive, vendors offer to encourage buyers to purchase larger quantities or meet specific purchasing targets. The rebate process defines, monitor, and modifies purchasing rebate agreements flexibly based on supplier, product, and volume-based purchasing commitments.

The Purchasing Rebate Processing scope item in SAP concerns the subsequent settlement of rebate conditions agreed upon with a supplier. Here’s a breakdown of the process and key components involved:

Condition Contracts Management

   - Rebate Agreements: Condition contracts are used to enter rebate agreements with suppliers. These contracts contain details about the agreed rebate conditions. In a condition contract, you specify organizational and process control parameters, business volume selection criteria, rebate conditions, and a settlement calendar.

Settlement Calendar

  - The calendar includes dates for delta accrual, partial settlements, and final settlements. Delta Accruals is a procedure where accruals are not posted with transactional documents but are posted subsequently in a specific settlement run. During partial and final settlements, the rebate calculation is performed based on the business volume and rebate conditions specified in the contract. Settlement documents are created to claim the rebate payments from the supplier.

Data Sources for Business Volume Determination

   - Supplier Invoices are generally used for amount-based rebate calculations. Material Documents are used for Goods receipt postings, usually applied for quantity or weight/volume-based rebate calculations.

In this article, we explore the two primary methods for settling condition contracts in the vendor rebate process within SAP S/4HANA, depending on the nature of the rebate agreement. When rebates are considered a cost reduction or gain from procurement activities, you perform a goods-related settlement as accounts payable, creating a credit memo with the supplier as the payer. This approach ensures that taxation is consistent with the taxation in the procurement process. Conversely, if the rebate agreement reflects a service rendered for the supplier, the settlement is performed as accounts receivable, generating an invoice for the supplier. Here, the taxation for the rebate amount is carried out in accordance with the service provided.

 

Additionally, there is a variant process concerning the transfer of settlement documents to accounting. Typically, settlement documents are created and immediately released to accounting. However, you can also employ a two-step condition contract to collect and post settlement documents from different condition contracts in a single journal entry to accounting. In this scenario, the reversal of accruals and customer revenues is not posted until the collective settlement document is created.

The Business Process diagram for Vendor Rebates is as below

Explain this with a real-world example let’s take the below retail business scenario

BestBuy Mart, a leading retail chain, negotiates a volume-based vendor rebate agreement with Crunchy Delights, a prominent snack food supplier. The agreement stipulates that if BestBuy Mart purchases over 50,000 units of a specific snack product within six months, they will receive a 5% rebate on the total purchase value exceeding 50,000 units.’

BestBuy Mart enters the rebate agreement details into SAP S/4HANA, specifying the product, time frame, and rebate percentage. Throughout the six months, BestBuy Mart places multiple purchase orders for the snack product, with SAP S/4HANA tracking all purchases linked to the rebate agreement. By the end of the six months, BestBuy Mart has purchased 60,000 units.

SAP S/4HANA calculates the rebate amount based on the 10,000 units exceeding the threshold, applying the 5% rebate rate to the total purchase value of these units. Subsequently, SAP S/4HANA generates a rebate credit memo for the calculated amount and processes the payment request to Crunchy Delights. BestBuy Mart utilizes SAP S/4HANA’s reporting tools to review rebate earnings and settlement details, ensuring transparency and accuracy in financial reporting.

In this article, we will discuss the Fiori applications used in the vendor rebate process in SAP S/4HANA.

Fiori app – “Monitor Supplier Condition Contracts”

Using this app we can Create new condition contracts, release condition contracts, and lock condition contracts for settlement. Also, users can monitor the life cycle of condition contracts and analyze relevant contract data from different perspectives.

Fiori App – "Display business volume – Condition Contracts"

In this application, using the Customer, Condition Contract, or Settlement Date can view the business volume data for the condition contracts

Fiori Application - "Settle Condition Contracts"

Using this app we can poste accruals according to the pricing conditions specified in the condition contract. Also to create Supplier credit memo (settlement document) and reverse the existing accruals we are using this application.

Fiori Application – "Collect Settlement Documents"

This application is use for Create Collective Documents from Final Settlement of the vendor rebates.

Using these applications you can precede the vendor rebates process in sap S4HANA starting from Condition contract creation, Release Supplier Condition Contract for Settlement, Check Business Volume, create partial and final settlements and approval and finally create the collect settlement documents.

This article aims to give a high-level understanding of vendor rebates and settlement in SAP S4HANA using Fiori applications. There are many pre-configurations and master data creation before this rebate process and in form next article I hope to discuss those with a complete overview. Please do share your comments. It will be very supportive of the next articles.

entry.title: Re: External Commission Settlement of Invoices created di...
entry.content:
Hi @AnustupSAP,

One the easiest option would be to use SAP HANA Studio to create a custom CDS view selecting from VWB2_VBRKVBRP_7. You will need to list all relevant fields for the new CDS view. Add as well the logic to capture only the relevant billing document with a delivery - you may want to exclude billing types for direct billing such as G2 and L2. 
The last step would be to assign the CDS view newly created to the relevant profile of business volume you're using.

The system will only create settlement with the relevant business volume based on your requirement. 

Regards, 
Gauthier VICTOR

entry.title: Standard network determination according to organization parameters
entry.content:
Requirement

We need to determine a standard project template according to organisational parameters like plant and sales organisation in assembly processing.

Solution mapping

We can cover this requirement in standard if we are going according to company code mapping for each requirement class, but if we have many plants and company codes, then it is not feasible to configure all according to requirement class and network type.

Here I am showing a way around for this requirement using a small implicit enhancement in Functional module CO_TA_TCN61_READ for Table TCN61.

After this enhancement you are able to create operative project according to plant and sales organization that I maintained in custom table that is basically a copy of standard table with extensions of two additional fields.

Custom Table

First, we need to copy table TCN61 into a custom table ZTCN61 so we can add our new fields in this table.

Go SE11 Transaction code and give table name and click on copy button.

Add fields below fields as Key fields in custom table.

 

Field

Key Field

Initial Value

Data element

Description

VKORG

X

X

VKORG

Sales Organization

WERKS

X

X

WERKS_D

Plant

 

 

Now Activate this table.

Maintenance View Setup

Here we need to maintained entry in this table for standard network selection so we need to create maintenance view and Transaction code for the same table.

Go to SE11.

Now again Click on save.

Transaction code Custom Table

Go to SE93

 Now Click on Save.

Material allocation to standard by Custom Table (ZTCN61)

Go to Transaction code ZCN08

 

Enhancement in Function Module CO_TA_TCN61_READ.

We are ready with custom table and new fields required for selection of standard network. Now we can enable enhancement code for selection of data from custom table instead of TCN61 table.

We have setup below Piece of code but as per coding standard it can be modified as required.

 

 

DATA: ls_ztcn61 TYPE ztcn61.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_vbak> TYPE any.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_vbap> TYPE any.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lt_xvbap> TYPE ANY TABLE.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lv_vkorg> TYPE vkorg.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lv_werks> TYPE werks_d.

 

ASSIGN ('(SAPMV45A)VBAK') TO <ls_vbak>.

ASSIGN ('(SAPMV45A)VBAP') TO <ls_vbap>.

 

IF <ls_vbak> IS ASSIGNED.

ASSIGN COMPONENT 'VKORG' OF STRUCTURE <ls_vbak> TO <lv_vkorg>.

ENDIF.

IF <ls_vbap> IS ASSIGNED.

ASSIGN COMPONENT 'WERKS' OF STRUCTURE <ls_vbap> TO <lv_werks>.

ENDIF.

 

IF <lv_vkorg> IS ASSIGNED AND <lv_werks> IS ASSIGNED.

  SELECT SINGLE *

  FROM ztcn61

  INTO ls_ztcn61

  WHERE matnr = matnr_imp

    AND auart = auart_imp

    AND vkorg = <lv_vkorg>

    AND werks = <lv_werks>.

  IF sy-subrc = 0.

    MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_ztcn61 TO tcn61_exp.

    EXIT.

  ENDIF.

ENDIF.

 

Testing of Process

 

Now we have completed the development and we need to test the process. For Testing we need to setup two different Project Templates in different plants.

Here I have setup different templates for individual Plant and sales organization but if you have multiple plants in same sales org that also we can used.

Projects Templates

For Plant 1010.

For Plant 1710

 

Note: - These templates are just for example for real scenarios I can different setup activities and WBS elements.

Material Master

Here we have setup same material master for both plants (1010 and 1710).

Sales Order and Operative Project Creation

Now we can create sales order for both plants and visualize the Operative project according respective plants that we used in sales order.

Sales Order for 1010

For Plant 1010 and Sales organization 1010.

Go Transaction code VA01 and create sales order.

 

 

Operative Project for 1010

Now Open the project created from sales order in CJ20N.

Go to Transaction code CJ20N.

Sales Order for 1710

For Plant 1710 and Sales organization 1710.

Go Transaction code VA01 and create sales order.

 

Operative Project for 1710

Now Open the project created from sales order in CJ20N.

Go to Transaction code CJ20N.

Conclusion

This enhancement is applicable to any situation in which the standard network should be derived based on parameters other than material master and network type.

 

Reference for the development

We would like to give big applause to the below blog writer from which I got the idea for this development.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/sap-ps-assembly-processing-standard-network-selection-based-on-parameters/ba-p/13279422

What could she do to get him to recognize her?

entry.title: Re: WIP revenue recognition
entry.content:
Hi Dilay, 

To view the current WIP account assignment you can perform the following steps in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition:

In your configuration environment, use the search function to open the following activity: Revenue Recognition. For more information, see Configuration Environment of SAP S/4HANA Cloud. For the Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition step, choose Configure. Accounts for revenue/cost adjustment, accruals and deferrals are configured in the assignment rules. For this, you will need to know, which assignment rule is used for your revenue recognition key. If you are unsure, which assignment rule is used, choose the dialog Recognition Keys and the sub structure Company Code and Accounting Principles. For example, for the revenue recognition key SPFC and for company code 1010 and accounting principle IFRS this is assignment rule COSTPOC. In the dialog Assignment Rules and sub dialog Assign Sources and Posting Rules you can find the corresponding accounts used for the corresponding sources.

Any change to this configuration needs to be carefully considered, ideally at the start of your implementation, as it will impact follow on postings. If in doubt please assess the situation with your advisory team.

If you are interested in how WIP is calculated, this again depends on the revenue recognition methods chosen. For further details, please refer to the extensive user assistance provided by SAP, which you can find here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/7fd9ad1108b94403a2871cf348d4b891.html

Regards,

Volkmar Zahn

SAP  S/4HANA Product Management

entry.title: Digital Transformation. View of an Enterprise Architect - Introduction
entry.content:
#GenerativeArt

SAP's announcement to discontinue maintenance and support for its established ERP systems puts the affected customers under pressure to transform (Schmeiler & Klassen, 2022, p. 4). At the same time, key technologies such as cloud computing and artificial intelligence are establishing themselves at breakneck speed, forcing companies to revise their business models to avoid becoming the workbench of more innovative companies.

Driven by these triggers, the digital transformation of the entire company is very often at the center of the discussion. Customers are seizing the opportunity to reshape traditional business management through innovative, intelligent and sustainable business processes. The new key technologies and tools enable freedom that can be used to increase the efficiency and productivity of the company. The established customer base can be expanded or completely new customer groups can be developed (Harwardt, 2022, p. 125).

Once the project decision has been made, however, the question of implementation arises. At this point, the initial course is already set for a successful project. A digital transformation requires a change in corporate culture, an adjustment of work processes and a realignment of the business strategy. It is therefore a measure that involves not only technology, but also people and culture. Nevertheless, in some projects, only IT is commissioned for this. Even though the IT application landscape represents a core element of the digital transformation, a dedicated view of IT means that the important perspectives of the specialist departments in particular are usually not sufficiently included. In order to meet these requirements, calls for an Enterprise Architect are becoming louder and louder. This inspired me to start a blog series about the typical discussions I often encounter. The series discusses the tasks of an enterprise architect and the requirements for a successful transformation project. In addition, the question is answered as to whether TOGAF as a tool of an enterprise architect helps to fulfill the requirements.

In the first part, I will shed light on the tasks of an enterprise architect and enterprise architecture management. In the second part, I will examine which criteria must be fulfilled for a successful transformation project. These criteria will be taken up in the third part and analyzed using TOGAF to determine the extent to which they can be supported by enterprise architecture management. Part 4 concludes with a process model that has already been successfully used by several customers.

 

Common thread of this blog series:

  1. Introduction 
  2. Tasks of an Enterprise Architect 
  3. Success Factors for Digital Transformation Projects 
  4. Help of an Enterprise-Architecture-Framework 
  5. Steps and Tips 

 

Bibliography:

Schmeiler, F., & Klassen, W. (2022). S/4HANA - die wichtigsten Chancen und Herausforderungen bei der Implementierung.

Harwardt, M. (2022). Management der digitalen Transformation: Eine praxisorientierte Einführung. Springer Fachmedien Wiesbaden. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-658-35918-8

entry.title: Digital Transformation. View of an Enterprise Architect - Tasks of an EA
entry.content:
#GenerativeArt

In the German-speaking world, discussions about enterprise architecture and enterprise architecture management often lead to confusion. In English, a distinction is made between 'enterprise architecture' as a structure and 'enterprise architecture management' as an activity. Both concepts are often translated into German in the literature with the term "Unternehmensarchitektur" which leads to a confusion between the activity and the structure (Keller, 2017, p. 23). Enterprise architecture as a term describes the representation of the IT architecture from the perspective of the people involved, whereas enterprise architecture management describes the processes, methods, tools, responsibilities and standards that are necessary for the enterprise architecture to fulfill its intended function.

If you try to capture the tasks of enterprise architecture management by defining the activity of the role, it quickly becomes clear that many different views come together here too. In contrast to other professions, there are no protected job titles for enterprise architects. In practice, this leads to creative and inconsistent role definitions and thus to ambiguities in the role profile and tasks. Various studies deal with the analysis and evaluation of job offers. In an evaluation of the Swiss job market, Thönssen and Dewitz found 156 job advertisements for 'Enterprise Architect'. However, when analyzing the content, they found that there was no common understanding of the tasks involved (Thönssen & Von Dewitz, 2018, p. 410). A study in the Norwegian healthcare sector came to a similar conclusion (Olsen, 2017, p. 638).

Another approach to identifying the tasks of an enterprise architect was carried out by Reselman. He analyzed various IT architect roles based on certifications. He formed four categories for this: Business-, Vendor-, Developer- and Operations-oriented architects. According to his analysis, the enterprise architect is a role within the business category and is responsible for ensuring that IT systems meet the organization's goals (Reselman, 2020a). Gellweiler also chose the path of evaluation via certifications, but came to the conclusion that the knowledge imparted in certifications is often not needed in practice: "[...] only a few jobs required knowledge of or certificates for a particular framework [...]" or that some role definitions such as TOGAF do not reflect the requirements of the market: "[...] that the widely used TOGAF framework [...] does not mirror the roles as searched on labor markets" (Gellweiler, 2020, p. 27).


In conclusion, it can be stated that the tasks of the enterprise architect cannot be clearly defined. In the literature, two areas of responsibility have emerged: on the one hand, the strategic level through the coordination of IT strategies with the specialist departments and, on the other, the tactical level, which is responsible for the planning of IT infrastructure systems and IT applications. Another level that is emerging is the combination of strategic and tactical levels with a focus on innovation and customer value. The Enterprise Architects are responsible for the strategic tasks within the company. Tactical tasks, such as the specification of hardware and software components and their interaction, are carried out by solution and software architects. In practice, the design of these roles and the associated tasks are individual to each company.

 

 

Common thread of this blog series:

  1. Introduction 
  2. Tasks of an Enterprise Architect 
  3. Success Factors for Digital Transformation Projects 
  4. Help of an Enterprise-Architecture-Framework 
  5. Steps and Tips 

 

Bibliography

Gellweiler, C. (2020). Types of IT Architects: A Content Analysis on Tasks and Skills. Journal of Theoretical and Applied Electronic Commerce Research, 15(2), 0–0. https://doi.org/10.4067/S0718-18762020000200103

Keller, W. (2017). IT-Unternehmensarchitektur: Von der Geschäftsstrategie zur optimalen IT-Unterstützung (3., überarbeitete und erweiterte Auflage). dpunkt.verlag.

Olsen, D. H. (2017). Enterprise Architecture management challenges in the Norwegian health sector. Procedia VIII Computer Science, 121, 637–645. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.procs.2017.11.084

Reselman, B. (2020a). What type of IT architect are you? https://www.redhat.com/architect/what-type-it-architect-are-you

Thönssen, B., & Von Dewitz, M. (2018). A Label is not enough – Approach for an Enterprise Architecture Role Description Framework. Procedia Computer Science, 138, 409–416. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.procs.2018.10.058

entry.title: Digital Transformation. View of a Enterprise Architect - Success Factors
entry.content:
#GenerativeArt

 

In order to analyze the extent to which an enterprise architecture framework as a tool of an enterprise architect can help to manage the digital transformation, the challenges and success criteria for such a project must first be identified.

If you search in sources such as Google Scholar for terms like "Critical Success Factors" in combination with "SAP", you will find a sufficient number of sources that have already dealt with this topic. The most frequently cited criteria are as follows:


1. Clear formulation of objectives, derived from the corporate strategy
The company's mission answers the question of what benefits the company brings to affected interest groups. The vision can be derived from this in order to define how this benefit intention can be implemented. The vision therefore includes the future business models, from which the IT strategy and necessary systems are derived.


2. Project Management
The core tasks of project management are the planning of all activities required for implementation and the management and control of the project in terms of deadlines and costs. Project management is also responsible for initiating measures in the event of problems arising.


3. Consideration of all perspectives
In SAP projects, project management is usually in the hands of IT (SAP S/4HANA 2022 study, 2022, p. 24). This often leads to a subjective view and is limited to system performance or the ability to integrate into existing system landscapes. Strategic aspects such as the development of new business models, customer centricity and greater transparency are neglected as a result. There is no alternative to including all perspectives.


4. Availability of resources
Transformation projects are often carried out in parallel with day-to-day business and therefore compete with it for employee resources. For a successful project, project roles must be planned and filled at an early stage in order to prevent difficulties in the project and disruption to day-to-day business. If external resources are used for project implementation, particular importance must be attached to sharing the knowledge and objectives of all project participants.


5. Benefits of standard software
Standard software maps business processes, but also offers enough flexibility within the standard to adapt it to your own processes. Companies therefore have the option of adapting the process to the solution or adapting the solution to the process. However, they tend to be highly individualized. This increases the implementation effort and leads to a higher susceptibility to errors (Brugger et al., 2021, p. 76).

 

6. Communication of changes
The introduction of new processes and technologies changes familiar procedures. The result is uncertainty among employees, which is just as important for specialist departments as for IT. To counteract this, timely and target group-oriented communication is important to promote acceptance and understanding of the change (Kohnke et al., 2005, p. 29; Menon, 2020, p. 88).

 

7. Silo Thinking
In order to achieve a corporate goal together, it is necessary to think and work in a holistic approach in end-to-end processes. However, the majority of companies are still trapped in functional silos with their associated command and control mindset, using structures from the 19th century. This is rooted in classic hierarchical thinking. The result is power struggles, conflicting goals and 'hidden agendas' (Brugger et al., 2021, p. 77). This can be broken up by a culture of collaboration, which is exemplified by the management level, as well as the active and early involvement of affected specialist departments.

 

8. Support of the management
Sustainable management support is crucial to the success of SAP implementations. It must fully drive forward the formulated goals and strategies and make key decisions as the project progresses (Brugger et al., 2021, p. 77). A lack of management involvement can have an impact on the commitment of key users, resulting in considerable additional work for the project management. Nevertheless, management often leaves the management of SAP projects to the IT department after the project decision has been made (Boban et al., 2016, p. 1338).

 

 

 

Common thread of this blog series:

  1. Introduction 
  2. Tasks of an Enterprise Architect 
  3. Success Factors for Digital Transformation Projects 
  4. Help of an Enterprise-Architecture-Framework 
  5. Steps and Tips 

 

 

Bibliograph

Boban, S., Stevanovic, V., Vujovic, V., & Denic, N. (2016). Key factors for successful implementation of ERP systems. Tehnicki Vjesnik - Technical Gazette, 23(5). https://doi.org/10.17559/TV-20150618213311

Brugger, T., Czeslik, M., Hager, A., & Uebel, M. (2021). Business Transformation mit S/4HANA: Leitlinien und Vorgehensmodell für einen ganzheitlichen Unternehmenswandel. Springer Fachmedien Wiesbaden. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-658-33968-5

Kohnke, O., Bungard, W., & Madukanya, V. (2005). Change Management im Rahmen von SAP-Implementie- rungsprojekten Eine empirische Studie.pdf. Gabler.

Menon, S. (2020). Critical Success Factors for ERP Projects: Recommendations from a Canadian Exploratory Study. International Journal of Business and Management, 15(2), 80. https://doi.org/10.5539/ijbm.v15n2p80

Studie SAP S/4HANA 2022. (2022). https://www.lufthansa-industry-solutions.com/de-de/studien/studie-2022- sap-s4hana

entry.title: SAP Adobe Form - Data Binding using Global Data and use of FM FP_FUNCTION_XML_INTERFACE
entry.content:
As per the Adobe LiveCycle Designer 10.0.1 for SAP documentation, A global field is a field used in a form where the same information, like an invoice number, needs to display in several places. By setting this field as global in the form design, we can easily reuse it wherever needed.

Global fields are very useful for repeating information in multiple places on a form. They help by reducing the amount of data sent and ensuring the same data appears consistently across the form.

Using the Global binding property, we can assign the same value to all form objects with the same name. When you make an object global, all objects with that name will share the same data during runtime. Because these global objects are linked to the same data value, changing the data in one global object updates it in all others with the same name.

Following table describes the details about global fields.

Point

Global fields

What happens

Designer shows the same value in all fields that have the same name.

Names of the fields

Names must be identical.

Run-time properties (such as the current page, number of pages, and current date/time)

You cannot add run-time properties to the field.

If you remove...

If you remove the global setting from one field, Designer removes the setting from all other fields with the same name.

Reference to nodes

Global fields can refer to nodes outside the current record.

Example of when to use

Use for information that is repeated in the form, such as customer name or address. The user enters the information once and the data automatically fills the other fields that are set to global.

Source: Adobe LiveCycle Designer 10.0.1 for SAP documentation

This blog explains how to use Function Module FP_FUNCTION_XML_INTERFACE instead of a normal form call and then binding the data to the fields using global data binding.

Pre-requisites steps to use FM: FP_FUNCTION_XML_INTERFACE

 This function module can be used as an alternative to the normal SAP adobe form call for global data binding, in which the input is the form name and data is in the XSTRING format. Function module details can be checked at: https://www.sapdatasheet.org/abap/func/fp_function_xml_interface.html

Implementation Steps:

  • Create an Interface.
  • Create a form.
  • Add Required fields and select binding as Global Data
  • Here, Field name is important as we are going to create an XML using the same field names. we can apply the global binding setting to the following types of objects:
  1. check boxes
  2. date/time fields
  3. drop-down lists
  4. image fields
  5. list boxes
  6. numeric fields
  7. decimal fields
  8. password fields
  9. radio buttons
  10. text fields
  11. barcoded fields (excluding Paper Form Barcode)
  • Create a driver program and add business logic to fetch the required data.
  • Convert the data to XML format.
  • Convert XML to XSTRING
  • Call FP_JOB_OPEN – Set output parameters.
  • Call FP_FUNCTION_XML_INTERFACE – Pass Form Name and XSTRING
  • Call FP_JOB_CLOSE  

Consider an example: A company wants to print a HU label consisting of a HU number, a QR code for HU, and a barcode for HU. Here, only one field is required, and that’s the HU number, and the same can be bind to the text field, barcode, and QR code.

Let’s create a form and add a HU number text field. The below pop-up will be displayed once the data binding is selected as global data. Click OK and enter the field name.

I have added the field name as HU_NUMBER. Same name to be used while binding the QR code and barcode.

Add QR code & Barcode and bind using the same name.

Selection Screen: Enter the HU Number

Output:

Below is the code snippet which shows how to use the FM FP_FUNCTION_XML_INTERFACE.

 

 

 

REPORT ztest.

DATA:lv_text         TYPE string,
     ls_outputparams TYPE sfpoutputparams,
     lv_docxml       TYPE xstring.

SELECTION-SCREEN: BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-000.
PARAMETERS: p_hu(8).
SELECTION-SCREEN: END OF BLOCK b1.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  "prepare XML
  CONCATENATE
  '<XML_DATA>'
  '<HU_NUMBER>'
  p_hu
  '</HU_NUMBER>'
  '</XML_DATA>'
  INTO lv_text.

  "Convert to XSTRING
  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_STRING_TO_XSTRING'
    EXPORTING
      text   = lv_text
    IMPORTING
      buffer = lv_docxml
    EXCEPTIONS
      failed = 1
      OTHERS = 2.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  ENDIF.

  IF lv_docxml IS NOT INITIAL.

    ls_outputparams-preview = abap_true.

    CALL FUNCTION 'FP_JOB_OPEN'
      CHANGING
        ie_outputparams = ls_outputparams
      EXCEPTIONS
        cancel          = 1
        usage_error     = 2
        system_error    = 3
        internal_error  = 4
        OTHERS          = 5.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    ENDIF.

    CALL FUNCTION 'FP_FUNCTION_XML_INTERFACE'
      EXPORTING
        formname       = 'ZTEST_F'
        /1bcdwb/docxml = lv_docxml
      EXCEPTIONS
        usage_error    = 1
        system_error   = 2
        internal_error = 3
        OTHERS         = 4.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    ENDIF.

    CALL FUNCTION 'FP_JOB_CLOSE'
      EXCEPTIONS
        usage_error    = 1
        system_error   = 2
        internal_error = 3
        OTHERS         = 4.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

 

 

In this way, we can use an XML-based form interface whenever we have to bind the same field in multiple places. This kind of framework is more suitable for requirements like printing certificates or printing labels where the number of fields are less in number but required to be bind in multiple places.

The preparation of XML can be made dynamic based on the requirements. A new table with fields like key and value can be created with TMG and used in the driver program to prepare XML dynamically. This will ease the process of adding new fields in the form.

I’m looking forward to hearing more about XML interface-based forms. Feel free to comment and discuss.

Best regards, thanks for reading, and stay healthy.
Abhijeet Tikar

entry.title: Digital Transformation. View of an Enterprise Architect - Help
entry.content:
#GenerativeArt

 

One of the main questions in the discussion about enterprise architecture is always whether an enterprise architecture framework helps to manage the digital transformation. To answer this question, I have analyzed the extent to which an EAF supports the success factors from Part 2. I used TOGAF as the reference framework, as it is currently the best-known and most widely used framework (Hanschke, 2022, p. 32; Reselman, 2020b).

 

1. Clear formulation of objectives
In order to ensure that the enterprise architecture to be developed follows the corporate goals and strategy, "board strategies, business plans, business strategy, IT strategy, business principles, business goals, and business drivers" are queried as input parameters (Josey & Hornford, 2022, p. 113). These serve as the basis for planning the architecture development and thus ensure that the architecture work is in line with the business objectives. Although this does not support the creation of the target formulation itself, its results are requested as input for the architecture work.

 

2. Project Management
TOGAF devotes an entire chapter to the topic of project management (TOGAF® Series Guide: Architecture Project Management, 2022). However, this relates purely to the integration of existing project management methods into the TOGAF process model. TOGAF therefore does not offer its own project management methodology "[...] on how to manage an Architecture-Project and leaves that to methods that focus on Project Management" (TOGAF® Series Guide: Architecture Project Management, 2022, p. 3).

 

3. Consideration of all perspectives
By dividing the enterprise architecture into different architecture domains, TOGAF forces the architecture to be viewed from different perspectives. Strategy, processes, organization and information requirements are considered in the business architecture. In particular, the interests of those involved with regard to the target architecture are taken into account here. The data architecture supports the structural aspects including the type of data processing. The application architecture focuses on the definition of the systems and their interaction, and the technology architecture designs a view of the infrastructure components as the basis for the implementation phase. Changes that occur during the sometimes very long project runtimes are managed by "architecture change management" and thus also taken into account. Combining these perspectives enables a holistic view of the organization and ensures that the business objectives are achieved.
Critically, however, it should not be forgotten that TOGAF was designed for the architectural development of information systems and technologies. Organizational culture or employee engagement, for example, are not considered.


4. Availability of resources
To carry out architecture development, employees with specific skills in the field of enterprise architecture work are required. These are often not available in the companies and must first be hired or trained. In addition, there is sometimes intensive involvement of relevant people during the project phase. This can lead to bottlenecks in day-to-day tasks or reduced availability of these resources in the architecture project. In line with this, considerable costs are incurred during the development and implementation of an enterprise architecture, which must also be planned and available as a resource for the project. Through clearly structured planning and implementation of enterprise architecture development in ADM and the associated clear definition of roles and responsibilities, TOGAF reduces these resource costs as much as possible.

 

5. Benefits of standard software
As an independent framework, TOGAF does not consider specific standard software. However, it provides a structured framework for the planning, development and implementation of IT architectures. This can be used to support the introduction of standard software and ensure integration into the overall architecture. By defining the architecture principles, processes and tools within ADM, the advantages and disadvantages of standard software are sufficiently analyzed.


6. Communication
TOGAF is a framework that helps with the structuring and planning of IT architectures. It creates transparency about the target state of the company to be achieved and can thus eliminate misunderstandings or concerns about the direction of the company. Through holistic stakeholder management, it involves those affected at an early stage and reduces uncertainty. However, since employee uncertainty is a highly complex, organizational issue, TOGAF alone is not sufficient in the author's opinion.

 

7. Silo Thinking
One of the key points of TOGAF is the involvement of all persons concerned in the architecture process. This promotes a culture of collaboration and breaks down barriers between different teams and departments. The different, standardized views of the business, data, application and technology architecture are combined in an overall architecture. This approach ensures the integration of all areas, resulting in the breaking down of silos. With TOGAF, architectural elements are reused across different parts of the organization, thus avoiding redundant or isolated solutions.

 

8. Management support
The TOGAF standard recommends the establishment and operation of an Enterprise Architecture Board to ensure that the organization acts in accordance with the principles set out in the architecture. This board must be able to make decisions and be held accountable for them. It should therefore be made up of management representatives from various interest groups. To ensure that the enterprise architecture board is involved in all major decisions, artifacts are created during various phases of ADM that must be signed by the enterprise architecture board in the form of architecture contracts. These include, for example, the architecture contract in phase A of the ADM or the architecture principles that are defined in the preliminary phase (Josey & Hornford, 2022, p. 138). This approach ensures sustainable management support.

 

 

In summary, the question of whether TOGAF helps with the digital transformation can be answered with a clear "Jein" in German or with an "it depends". TOGAF is a very comprehensive and generic framework that supports 5 of the 8 success factors. However, it should not be forgotten that this holistic approach also leads to a high level of complexity and rigidity, which requires a lot of time and resources. Spontaneous use is therefore not possible (Hanschke, 2022, p. 36). If you come across the argument in discussions that there is tailoring for this, you must also bear in mind that individual tailoring is itself a project and can only be carried out by a resource that already has extensive experience in dealing with TOGAF.

So its use must be considered on an individual basis and makes no sense if the use of TOGAF takes up more time than the complete rest of the project.

If it is fine for you to use a Vendor-Specific Framework, I would recommend to have a look at the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework (SAP EAF). Within SAP EAF, TOGAF forms the basis for the phases, activities and artifacts but SAP EAF extends the TOGAF metamodel with SAP-specific concepts and offers additional methods and tools that have been specially developed for SAP integration. You can find a description here. 

 

 

Common thread of this blog series:

  1. Introduction 
  2. Tasks of an Enterprise Architect 
  3. Success Factors for Digital Transformation Projects 
  4. Help of an Enterprise-Architecture-Framework 
  5. Steps and Tips 

 

 

 

Bibliograph

Hanschke, I. (2022). Enterprise Architecture Management – einfach und effektiv: Ein praktischer Leitfaden für die Einführung von EAM. Carl Hanser Verlag GmbH Co KG.

Josey, A., & Hornford, D. (2022). The TOGAF® Standard, 10th Edition—A Pocket Guide. The Open Group.

Reselman, B. (2020b, September 14). TOGAF and the history of enterprise architecture. https://www.redhat.com/architect/togaf

entry.title: Digital Transformation. View of an Enterprise Architect - Steps and Tips
entry.content:
#GenerativeArt

 

In 2021, I had already written a blog on the subject of architecture development, in which I wrote down my customer experiences during these days (Blog). As this approach is still valid today, I have integrated an updated version of this blog into the series. It is based on TOGAF and the SAP EAF. 

Note: No licenses are required for the following procedure! If you already have licenses for SAP Signavio or LeanIX, for example, this makes the architecture work much easier

 

The following methodology is intended to serve as a lean, evolutionary option for architecture development, which primarily includes the active participation and cooperation of several parties, instead of relying exclusively on existing experts and wisdom. Because of this, a corresponding project should be viewed and accompanied from the perspective of the following business units:

 

Specialist Department


“Applications are computer programs that perform useful functions for the user” (Wikipedia).
The user must define in which business processes, sub-processes of his activity the software should support him (which capabilities must the application include?).

 

Project Sponsor (Board of Directors, Management)


The development of a target architecture must be based on the support of senior management:

Guiding Principles
Reliable fundamental decisions must be made (e.g., what is the company’s cloud strategy?) .

Personnel expenditure
In total, a large number of employees are usually tied up in the project (even if not always full-time). This personnel expenditure must be known and approved.

Innovation pressure
Some customers take the target architecture as an opportunity to align their IT with it. For this, decisions must be made about the future direction of the company (e.g., from product to service provider).

 

IT-Department

Introduction/Operation

Which applications (modules) are necessary?

How/where should they be operated?

 

Migration (not part of the consideration of this blog)

Which transactions still exist, are no longer necessary or have been simplified?

What needs to be considered for the respective modules, functions and transactions?

Examination of effects of the “custom code“? Changes in operation?

Which functionalities can be found in which applications?


This list is not intended to be complete, but it illustrates the importance of each role/viewpoint for the success of the project.

 

The following steps show an example of a methodical procedure for developing a high-level target architecture based on the business processes used. A step-by-step approach, from the process to the correct technology (top-down). If all parties agree on the high-level architecture that has been developed, the next phase is to start refining/strengthening this architecture.

 
 

 

 

Procedure

 

 

Setting the Stage

What are we actually working on here?
All parties involved must be clear in every meeting about the expected goal before the target architecture can be worked out. For example, if one party in the room thinks we are working out the target architecture for the “SAP S/4HANA PoC” and the other assumes an “SAP S/4HANA Enterprise Architecture 2025” (which is exactly what happened to me, by the way), this can lead to very entertaining discussions.


NOTE:
1. The Goal: Define the goal concisely. Repeat the goal definition before each meeting to ensure clarity.

2. The Focus: We want to identify the correct technology via the process and the associated requirements. The currently used software or even the technical migration path must not play a role in this process 


Guiding/Architecture Principles

Guiding/Architecture Principles are guidelines or fundamental decisions for the project. These are decisions that everyone thinks are clear anyway. On closer questioning, however, everyone has a different view of these “clear” rules.

Rule examples: Cloud first, data is an asset, data protection and privacy are key, one central ERP system, etc.

NOTE:
Write down these rules. Designate a responsible person for every single rule (preferably a sponsor) who will take responsibility for them. Make these rules known to all participants and avoid that these rules are discussed anew in every meeting. Otherwise they will (you guessed it)….never get done.

 

 

Step 1 – Identification of the End-2-End business process


The identification of the business process(es) for which a target architecture is to be developed is elementary. Without this step, you will not be able to appoint the necessary experts in the company for the subsequent steps.

The “SAP Business Accelerator Hub”, with its SAP reference processes, supports the identification of the correct End-2-End business process: SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

 

The outcome of this step should be to narrow down the End-2-End business processes to be considered.

 

 

Step 2 – From End-2-End process to business activity


Often, an entire End-2-End process with all its activities is not to be included in the consideration. The reference processes of the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and their associated documentation also serve to delimit the process.

The next levels of the End-2-End process (see the following Value-Flow-Diagram) help to identify the correct sub-process (modular), the business process segment, and the associated business activity (example of the End-2-End process “Plan to Fulfill”)

 

NOTE:
Identification down to activity level is usually very difficult to implement on the sole basis of a Value-Flow-Diagram. Ask your sales representative for support and organize a workshop between SAP and the process owners in which the overall or partial process is represented in the system.

 

 

Step 3 – Identification of the relevant applications and their relationship


Up to this point, the focus has been on identifying the business process, sub-process and activity, and thus on the perspective of the business department. The next step is to transfer this result to the IT perspective and thus to the selection of the correct applications based on the result of the previous steps.

The “Scenario Implementation” point leads to a Solution-Architecture-Diagram (End-2-End business process), which shows the necessary components and their relationship to each other:

If a business process is not to be used in its entirety, the application belonging to the sup-process down to the activity-level can be identified starting from the Value-Flow-Diagram via the Software-Collaboration-Diagram.

 

 

Step 4 – The Platform


The previous steps represent a simple way of identifying the required applications based on the business processes used. Furthermore, it must be remembered that there are also key issues that may not be considered through this route but have a potential impact on the overall architecture.

Integration: An end-to-end process normally involves several applications that have to interact with each other. Even if the interaction between standard components is usually predefined, there are always individual integrations. Consider, for example, the connection of non-SAP systems. To identify the right integration software, I recommend using the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISAM). 

Development: If a standard process cannot reflect reality, there are two possibilities. Adaptation of reality to the standard process or adaptation of the standard process to reality. A common guiding principle is “back to standard”, which means adapting reality to the standard process. However, there are always exceptions in which this is not possible for good reason. In order to have the right tool at hand for these exceptions, it is advisable to use the SAP Application Extension Methodology (AEM). 

Data&Analytics: The potential to gain value from data remains largely untapped as companies struggle to keep pace with simply accessing, storing and harmonizing the data in complex and unintegrated data landscapes. To provide guidance in the design and validation of solution architectures for data-driven business innovations I recommend to use the SAP Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology (DAAM). 

 

Further potential questions to think about:

  • What is my master data strategy?
  • How does my master data organisation look like?
  • What are my financial consolidation and planning requirements?
  • Do I need a solution for government communication?
  • Does the architecture meet the requirements of the DSGVO (GDPR)?
  • Have I thought about the Corporate Sustainability Reporting?

Again, this list is not intended to be complete, but rather thought provoking. Further ideas, including solution diagrams, can be found via SAP Missions:

 

 

Step 5 – The Unification


Different applications as well as their relation have now been identified via one or mostly several processes/subprocesses and platform requirements. In order to create and display a customer-specific architecture diagram from this, I recommend using the SAP Cloud Platform Solution Diagrams&Icons: Be Visual! 

 

 

Conclusion

This is a very practical solution for identifying the correct application starting from a business process and creating a high-level target architecture. Involve SAP early on to implement this methodology. Joint planning with experts from the customer and SAP right from the start facilitates the development of an architecture and identifies any stumbling blocks early on.

 

 

Common thread of this blog series:

  1. Introduction 
  2. Tasks of an Enterprise Architect 
  3. Success Factors for Digital Transformation Projects 
  4. Help of an Enterprise-Architecture-Framework 
  5. Steps and Tips

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account and getting started in the community
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

Thank you for putting together this demo and "how to" for the Digital Skills Initiative program.  I tried to join the beginners community group but was met with an error "You do not have sufficient privileges for this resource or its parent to perform this action" so unfortunately I was not able to comment in your referenced blog.

I feel that I would be an ideal candidate for this opportunity as I fall under numerous of the guidelines in your applicant guide.  I'm a single parent transitioning to a new career, or at least trying to, who was diagnosed with ADD as an adult back in 2020.  My career opportunities locally are sparce because of the area I live in, a remote opportunity would help counter this.  My background in SAP coming up from the production floor into production planning and supply chain has given me lots of on hand experience but without certifications or a degree my advancement has been limited.  I'm a first generation college student as well, currently trying to go back for an information systems degree to potentially help bolster my chances with an SAP certification.  Shortly after leaving the military I went to work for a company that was eventually bought out by Siemens as a temporary employee and worked my way up the ladder learning a few ERP systems along the way but helping become one of our SAP super users to help train others during our SAP implementation.

I've worked with BAAN, NetSuite, Oracle, Microsoft GP, and SAP and out of all of them prefer SAP and what it has to offer.  Having experience with day to day operations with SAP from the production side, production planning, procurement, and even helping and being a key Super User in ~4 implementations with different companies would make me a standout candidate to become certified and allow me to expand on the knowledge I've already gained on the job so I can learn more about what SAP has to offer and what my potential could be with certifications.

I enjoy seeing how the SAP system can improve processes and the impact technology has on businesses that operate on it.  I've worked at a few facilities who have all incorporated it in different ways and I was able to take key parts from each setup and try to build upon them at the next facility. 

I'm eager to start learning more about SAP commerce, SAP S/4Hana Sourcing and Procurement, and SAP S/4 Hana Production Planning and Manufacturing.  I want to build upon the skills I already have been honing and use them to become a consultant within the SAP community.  I know that business requirements change and technology changes so I want to stay up-to-date with the latest SAP advancements and what they have to offer.  I'm looking forward to this opportunity and exited about the possibilities the future holds.  Thank you for considering my application.

Best Regards,

Bryan

entry.title: Re: F1520 Proposed Posting Date Field during Reprocessing of Bank Statement Items
entry.content: Maybe I have misunderstood the note, but #1 in the note says that if the bank file contains a date, then that becomes the proposed posting date. In our case, the bank file does have a date and that is the date we want as our proposed posting date in the reprocessing bank statement item. FEBAN does exactly that. It uses the bank statement date as the posting date when reprocessing items. We simply want the app to do the same.

entry.title: Re: SAP DM, REO - Opeation Split with Order POD (Relaxed...
entry.content:
Hi,

Operation Split in REO is currently used for planning and scheduling purposes at REO level only, and don’t really reflect on the amount/quantity of SFCs or Charges released at all at the moment.

You should be able to use some combination of Material Lot Size, Production Processes, Public APIs, Automatic Triggers  / Business Rules and POD Plugins to meet your split requirements.

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Hide Redemption Tile
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

I've read the KB regarding the hiding of the Redemption Tile, however I think this would only work if the user will not receive any Points from a Point-Based Award Program at all.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3397410/E

Our client requires the use of both Milestone and Point-Based Programs.

However, our client's redemption process will happen outside of SF, hence this tile needs to be hidden from the end-user side. The total points received are also being converted into their currency, however it would not make sense as it is supposed to show "points" instead of the "currency".

 

I tried hiding the Spot Award User Balance from the RBP, but when I clicked the details of the award under Awards History, the page only keeps on loading. The MDF object is also not configurable at the moment. 

I also tried checking if we can modify this in the language translations, but there is no token associated to the currency.

Would it be possible to completely hide this tile, given our scenario? Is there still a way to work around this?

Thanks so much.

entry.title: Fiscal Year Variant
entry.content:
Fiscal Year Variant:

Defines Financial Year, Defines how many normal periods and special periods for the financial year.

Normal Financial year contains 12 normal periods plus up to 4 Special Periods

Normal periods always start from period1 to 12

Normal periods contains the period definitions.

Special periods does not contains the period definitions

Period definition :

It defines each period start date and end date of the period

Example

Period1--->from April1 to April 30th

Period2 ----> From 1st May to 31st May

Period12-----> From 1st March to 31st March

Type of the Fiscal Year:

1.Calendar Fiscal Year

2.Non Calendar Fiscal Year

1.Calendar Fiscal Year:

Those Financial Year Posting Period should be equal to Calendar Month.

Example: Period1 = Jan , Period2 = Feb , Period3 = Mar , Period4 = Apr , Period5 = May , Period6 = June , Period7 =July Period12 = Dec

Every Period Start Date and End Date should be equal to Calendar Month that type of Financial Year can be called as Calendar Year.

Period1----> 1st jan to 31st jan

Period2----> 1st Feb to 28th feb or 29th Feb

Period3 ----< 1St March to 31st March

 

Period12----> 1st Dec to 31st Dec

Note :

Not required to define any period definition

System follows standard functionality if FY defined / activated with Calendar year indicator

In the Calendar Fiscal Year, the Posting Periods and Fiscal Year Determines based on Posting Dates

Posting date : 20.03.2022======> system determines Posting Period =3 and Fiscal Year =2022

Not required any mapping rules for period definitions in case of FY as a calendar year

2.Non Calendar Fiscal Year:

Other than Jan to Dec FY can be called as Non Calendar year

Example :  April to March

                 July to June

                 Oct to Sept

Even though FY follows Jan to dec but period definition not equal to calendar month start date and end date

FY====> 1st Jan to 31st Dec

Period 1------> 1st Jan to 29th Jan             not equal to calendar Month # Jan

Period2-------> 30th Jan to 28th Feb          not equal to calendar Month # Feb

Period3-------> 29th Feb to 30th March      not equal to calendar Month # March

Note :

Posting date : 20.03.2022====> How the system knows what is the posting period = ???? What is the Fiscal year = ???

in case of non calendar FY , Need to specify the period definitions to get the Posting period and FY

In case of Non-Calendar Year ,the Posting Period and Fiscal Year determine based on posting date along with Fiscal Year Variant Configuration Settings

Definition :

In Case of Non-Calendar Year, we need to maintain relationship between Calendar Month and Calendar Day and Posting Period 

 Calendar monthCalendar Day FY PeriodYear Shift Indicator
Jan13110-1
Feb22911-1
Mar33112-1
Apr4301 
May5312 
Jun6303 
July7314 
Aug8315 
Sept9306 
Oct10317 
Nov11308 
Dec12319 

Year Shift Indicator:

in case of Calendar FY====> Not applicable year shift indicator

in case of Non Calendar FY====> applicable / required year shift indicator

Calendar FY 

 Example 2021----> Period1 (01.01.2021 to 31.01.2021) …….. Period12 (01.12.2021 to 31.12.2021)

in the above case , FY Periods and calendar FY are identical

Calendar Year ===>2021=====> FY 2021===> Periods and calendar months are identical

In the above case , not required year shift indicator functionality

Non calendar FY

Few periods belong to One calendar year and few periods belongs to other calendar year

Example : FY 2021 Apr to March

1101.02.2022 to 29.02.2022-1
1201.03.2022 to 31.03.2022-1

Business requirement===> FY 2022=====> Oct 2021 to Sept2022

Example : Define FY Oct to Sept

 Calendar MonthCalendar DayFY periodYear Shift Indicator

Note :

Need to use year shift indicator to bring the different calendar year periods under one Fiscal year for non calendar FY.

Maintain Fiscal Year Variant:

SPRO -> SAP Reference IMG -> Financial Accounting -> Financial Accounting Global Settings -> Ledgers -> Fiscal Year and Posting Periods -> Maintain Fiscal Year Variant (OB29)

Maintaining standard FV for Company Code

Assign Company code to a Fiscal Variant:

SPRO -> SAP Reference IMG -> Financial Accounting -> Financial Accounting Global Settings -> Ledgers -> Fiscal Year and Posting Periods -> Assign Company Code to a Fiscal Variant (OB37)

Note: Creating our own Fiscal variants

Z1 --> FY var for Calendar Year -->  Jan to dec

Z2 --> FY Apr to March

Period Text=====> Its optional

==> Possible to define our own text both in English , Local languages

==> It follows the standard text if not maintained your own text

 

Year Independent FY Variant:

Can be defined either calendar year or Non calendar year

FY period definition is constant irrespective of the Fiscal year

Period definition it wouldn't vary depends on the FY

Example :

FY===> Apr to march

FY===> Jan to Dec

FY===> July to June

FY===> Oct to Sept

 

 Calendar monthCalendar Day FY PeriodYear Shift Indicator
Jan13110-1
Feb22911-1

                                                  FY ===> Apr to March

Note : In the above case , the above period definition not specified any specific to FY===> these type of FY can be called as year Independent FY

Year dependent FY Variant:

Possible to define only Non calendar years

FY period definition is not constant and period definition it may vary depends on the Fiscal year

Period definition it may vary depends on the FY

Example1 :Define FY Jan to Dec and every period should end with last working day of the calendar month only Sunday is Holiday.

1231120  ===> 01.12.2024 till 31.12.2024

Short End Fiscal Year:

Those fiscal year posting periods less than the 12 calendar months can be called as shortened fiscal year.

Fiscal year Conversions ( Specific year conversion)

      Business Use New case :

            Newly incorporation of a company or newly starting a business during the year.

            Winding up and merging of the company during the year, the specific year becomes the shortened              FY

    Shifting from Calendar year to Non Calendar Year or Vice versa.

            FY Jan to dec====> FY Oct to Sept

            FY Jan to dec====> FY APR to March

   Shifting from Non Calendar year to Calendar Year

          FY Oct to Sept====> FY Jan to Dec

         FY Apr to March ====> FY Jan to Dec 

Business Example :

Business need to shift from Jan to dec into APR to March

FY2020Jan 2020 to Dec 2020   ======> Full Financial YearYear dependent FY
FY2021Jan 2021 to  March 2021  ======> Shortened  FYYear dependent FY
FY2022APR 2021  to March2022   ======> Full Financial YearYear dependent FY
FY2023APR 2022  to March2023   ======> Full Financial YearYear dependent FY

Process steps to be followed

1.Need to complete all the period end activities

   Month end closing process to be completed

   FI Period end process

   CO period end process

2.Apply the FY conversions

   Use the Same FY variant code ( example Y8)

   Need to activate Year dependent FY indicator , Short FY indicator for FY variant Y8 for the year 2021

   Need to define Year dependent FY variant for year 2021

   Need to define Year dependent FY for future atleast to 10 years to 20years ( like 2022, 2023, 2024 till 2041)

   Need to define Year dependent FY for past atleast 10years to 20 years back ( like 2019,2018,2017,2016,2015 -------->1990)

3.Need to perform settings in Asset accounting

  Asset Accounting Configuration relating to Fiscal year change

   Step1: Define Reduction rules for shortened Fiscal Year

   Path: SPRO->Financial Accounting->Asset Accounting->Valuation->Fiscal Year->Shortened Fiscal Years->Define Reduction Rules for Shortened Fiscal Years

   Step2: Generate Period Control Rules

   Path: Financial Accounting->Asset Accounting ->Depreciation->Valuation Methods->Period Control->Generate Period Controls OA84

  Step3: No reduction is Short year for depreciation key

  Path: SPRO->Financial Accounting->Asset Accounting->Valuation->Depreciation->Valuation Methods->Depreciation Key->Maintain Depreciation Key AFAMA

4.Need to perform period end process again FI & CO

Difficulties :

Dump / run time errors in the settlement of orders / asset accounting

These issues resolved by applying the SAP OSS ( Support) notes

Current FY ====> Calender Year as below

Now need to shift from Jan to dec to April to march from year 2021 onwards

     FY conversions to be defined as a Year dependent FY

     Base year to be defined as Shorted FY

     FY 2021====> Jan 2021 till March 2021====> No of periods==>3Periods

Save 

FY 2020

FY2020===> Jan 2020 to Dec 2020

Save

Do not save then click on Fiscal year Variant

Maintain for year 2022

Do not save then click on Fiscal Year variant

Save

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extension
entry.content:
Since SAP Cloud ALM  was released to customers, back in July 2020, it was only possible to get one SAP Cloud ALM tenant per eligible customer number.

 

Lots of customers have requested to get multiple tenants for the same customer number, for various reasons:

  • Multiple productive tenants:
    • To provide SAP Cloud ALM to different organizational units in large enterprises.
    • To separate implementation and operations.
    • …
  • Adding a development or test tenant to the existing productive tenant
    • To build and test extensions.
    • …
  • To separate systems from different landscapes:
    • For systems containing sensitive information.
    • …
  • To have training or playground environments.
  • And much more.

I am glad to announce that SAP has been working on this. We will release soon a new product on the SAP price list called SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extension so that customers will be able to subscribe to additional tenants for SAP Cloud ALM.

I will refer from here on to the standard SAP Cloud ALM tenant as the tenant which is granted to the customers as part of Enterprise Support, cloud editions, in SAP Enterprise Support and in Product Support for Large Enterprises (full details can be found here: usage rights).

Here is a summary of what you will get per extension subscribed:

  • The entitlement to a fully productive SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • From a functional and technical point of view, this tenant is identical to the standard SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • A baseline of 8 GB SAP HANA Memory.
  • A baseline of 8 GB monthly outbound API data transfer.
  • The possibility to provision a Tricentis test automation tenant integrated with your extra tenant.

The usage rights are similar to the ones from the standard SAP Cloud ALM tenant, except for first bullet point of the usage rights (the one about the support agreements).

There is no restriction on the number of tenants which you can subscribe to. You can get as many as needed for your organization and purposes.

Once the SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extension has been purchased, you will be able to provision your extra tenant(s) from SAP for Me just like the standard SAP Cloud ALM tenant, and the process will be fully automated as well.

Lab Preview - Might not reflect final state of the product. 

 The SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extension has been announced in the SAP roadmap. It is currently planned for Q3 2024 (This is the current state of the planning and it can be changed by SAP at any time), therefore it is coming very soon.

Stay tuned for more information.

entry.title: Inconsistency in viewing the Award History
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

Our client requires the use of Point-Based Award Programs, however their redemption will happen outside of SF. The behavior that we noticed is that the view in Award History is not consistent for the following scenarios:

 

1. Awards History > My Team - award value is showing in "points"

 

2. Awards History > Myself > Received - award value is showing in "currency"

 

3. Awards History > Myself > Sent - award value is showing in "points"

 

The same behavior is observed when viewing the details of each award. Could we please check if there is a way to show that these awards are in "points" instead of "currency"?

It would be a confusing for end-users to show these in currencies as they should only see the total points they received.

 

Thank you,

Karelyn

entry.title: SAP MY Inbox Issue with SAP Fiori launchpad multiple systems ( S4 ECC and CRM )
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Need your expert advice here please -

Background:

  • We  have one MyInbox and processes workflows initiated in CRM as well as S4.
  • CRM System Alias was used to enable one MyInbox for both the systems.
  • Realization today that a manager must have an ID in both the systems or MyInbox has errors.
  • We have many managers who do not have a CRM id
  • Managers are not real SAP users but they receive mail with a link which navigates them to MyInbox for approvals.

Plans:

  • Disable CRM Alias to allow Managers to approve S4 workflows
  • Copy MyInbox so we will have two MyInboxes (one for S4 workflows and one for CRM workflows)

Where we are:

  • CRM Alias has been disabled
  • We are having challenges creating a secondary MyInbox for CRM workflows

What is needed:

  • Requesting your help in how to create second CRM MyInBox  or suggest alternatives for CRM workflows. 

Thanks & Best Regards,

Sunit

entry.title: Re: TRUNCATE TABLE PARTITION takes hours when deleting an...
entry.content:
A possible explanation, if the table has global index(s), is that TRUNCATE TABLE <table-name> PARTITION <partition-name> causes all global indexes to be rebuilt, even if the target partition is empty.  CR 829608 was opened for this a few days ago; there isn't yet a KBA for it.

A debugging approach for finding out what a process is doing is to use "dbcc stacktrace(spid)" to find out what internal functions are currently being executed.  Run it multiple times for a long-running process to get a profile of what functions are involved.  The function names are sometimes cryptic, but usually some sense of what is going on can be extracted from them.  If the top of the stack shows function names including "idx" on "indx", for instance, would point towards index building, while functions names including "oam" or "gam" would indicate ASE was searching through OAM or GAM structures to find extents belonging to the object.

To use dbcc stackstrace, first use sp_who to find the spid of the process performing the TRUNCATE, then substitute in that spid in

set switch on 3604 -- causes internal dbcc command outputs to be sent to client
go
dbcc stacktrace (spid)
go

Cheers,
-bret

entry.title: Automatic Schema Assignment for Vendor Types in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I need assistance with configuring automatic schema assignment during vendor creation in SAP S/4HANA. My client has two types of vendors: Local and Foreigner. They want the system to automatically assign the appropriate schema based on the vendor type selected.

Specifically, when creating a Foreigner vendor and selecting the Foreigner vendor group, the system should automatically fill the schema field with 'ZF'. Conversely, when creating a Local vendor and selecting the Local vendor group, the system should automatically fill the schema field with 'ZL'.

From my understanding, this functionality is not available in the standard configuration. Could you please guide me on how to achieve this requirement? Any advice on configuration or customisation steps would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Configure the Employee Central Benefit Type - Reimbursements; for Beginners Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:

I would like to share my wonderful experience towards the “Global Employee Benefits” to the Beginners of Employee Central Consultant who has passionate on the Benefits Topic.

Most of them will not get the opportunity to know about the Benefits Topic/Product Features and they will not be in the comfortable zone to discuss the features in the Implementation Project > Specifically in the Fit to Standard Workshop.

The idea here is to enable the Consultant to know the basic overview of the “Benefit” topic and start discussing the same with Customer (in the Project to make use of this module and provide the extended user experience).

It is highly recommended to suggest the Customer to use the SAP SuccessFactors Best Practices and Approaches to strengthen the HR Process in the Organization.

The following are the Benefit Types in SuccessFactors.

  1. Allowance
  2. Reimbursement
  3. Pension/Savings/Retirement Plan
  4. Insurance
  5. Wallet
  6. Deductible Allowance
  7. Savings Plan
  8. Others

Most of the countries extensively can use the “Reimbursement/Claim and Insurance Types since it has the features to automate End-to-End Business Process for the customers.

As a beginner, you must talk about the benefits module and how this can be helpful for the HR Business Process, how they can strengthen HR Process and automate the claim process with end-to-end process without manual intervention.

Let us start with the easy one (Reimbursement Benefit Type), How to Configure the Business Process, How to Configure the Simple Business Rule and Enable the Role Based Permissions.

Business Requirements:

Our customer is having the following requirements / cases.

  • Air Ticket Reimbursement (this is used as a Sample Cases to share the look and feel of the configurations and understand the configurations steps)
  • 2 Level of Approval Process (Workflow)
  • Restriction or Validations by Using the Business Rule
  • Custom UI Screen High level Screen Shot
  • User Experience

Here, we are going to see the step-by-step configuration process and how to enable this functionality to Business User and Employees (Including the Workflow and Business Rules)

Section 1: Workflow and Business Rule Configuration

 

Note: You can add the validations based on the business needs. Here I have restricted with claim ID.

Note: There are two level of approvals been configured, this needs to be deployed into the benefits object (Under Validate and Save Rules respectively) as per the below screen shots.

Section 2: Step-by-Step Configuration Process

Goto > Benefit Admin Overview

Step 1 & Step 2 Specify the Benefit Type and General Information’s

Select the Benefit Type from the Dropdown Value (Reimbursement)

Enter the General Information’s as above Benefit ID, Name, and Effective from Date and Choose the Relevant Currency to display in the claim)

In Eligibility Criteria, always the entitlement amount should be -1.

In Payroll Integration Choose from the Dropdown Values (Integration with Payroll or Benefits)

Step 3: Associate the Legal Entities

Here, you have to choose and associate the listed Legal Entity which are eligible for this reimbursement.

If there more legal entities, but only few of them are eligible to claim this process, in this case you have to choose the relevant legal entities from the list. So that it will be showed only those entities employees.

Step 4: Specify Enrollment and Claim Dates

Create the Benefit Schedule, Select the Frequency from the Dropdown Values and Days Remaining to Claim into Show.

Step 5 & 6: Define Enrollment Details & Add Useful Contacts

Choose, whether enrolment required or now, here we let us use no required.

Choose the Contact Person from the List (or create it by adding.

Step 7: Hyperlinks and Policy Documents

The below steps will be very useful the end user to view the Policy Documents, Links, URL etc. towards the reimbursements (you can add the policy documents based on the different reimbursement)

Step: 8 & 9 Specify Claim / Reimbursement Details (if Benefit Type is Reimbursement)

Here, you need to choose the Claim Screen ID (Standard), if you have configured any custom UI, please select the UI to display into Users.

Note: There are some customers, might ask for more fields in the claim screen and wants to validate or add etc. so you have to ensure the new fields in the object level and create the UI accordingly and specify the respective claim screen ID as highlighted above.

In Benefit Payment Options, Choose the Pay Component which needs to be aligned for the Reimbursement, Choose the Payment Mode (Payroll), Ensure the Default into Yes.

Step 10: Other Options

There are below exception options towards the benefit for exceeded amount and approval exceptions, let us not look it now and go without selections.

Configurations Steps are completed now, and you must enable the Role Based Permission to provide access to the Employees, and Approvers respectively.

Section 3: One of the Sample UI (Customized Solution) which mentioned in the step 8.

 

Section 4: User Experience: In Employee / Administrator View, User will create the claim as per the below screen shots.

Approver’s View

I hope, the above information will be useful for the community and start their journey towards the Benefits Module (Business Requirements, Workflow, Business Rules and Validations, and how to deploy the same into Configure Object Definition along with User Experience.

Kindly request you all to share your and valuable feedback and suggestions to strengthen the blog and motivate me to do such more blogs.

Thank You.

Anba ( Anbazhagan N)

entry.title: EC Time Off Rounding of Accruals - Detailed Example
entry.content:
Introduction

Business rules in time off can be tricky based on the customers' requirements, and rounding of accruals may be one of them. There are couple of notes and blogs regarding this topic. (The most generic one can be found here.) In this article, I wanted to share a bit more complicated example with detailed explanations. You will see that the business rule is straight-forward easy; and compared to that, the scenario requires more attention.

Scenario
In our example, employee earns total of 20 days of accrual within a year. We want to have a hire and/or a termination rule, and want to recalculate accruals based on couple of conditions.

If the employee worked for a full year, the accrual will be 20 days, and will be prorated based on applicable months. As an example, for 10 months of work, the prorated amount will be: 20 / 12 x 10 = 16,67.

Note: The calculation of the total number of months within a year where employee was applicable for accrual is not the subject of this article. This value will be stored in a standard variable, which will be used within a business rule.

By looking at the example above, we can then create a table as follows:

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,673,3356,678,331011,6713,331516,6718,3320

Now comes the tricky part: The customer wants to change its approach and adjust these amounts, so that:

  1. Decimals below 0,25 should be rounded to 0.
  2. Decimals between 0,25 to 0,75 should be rounded to 0,5.
  3. Decimals above 0,75 should be rounded to 1.

So the desired accrual amounts will be as follows:

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,53,556,58,51011,513,51516,518,520

The following section will be about how to achieve this.

Implementation

The business rule that we are going to use can be found below.

Now let us breakdown the rule in details.

  1. The number of months is our variable as mentioned before, and this stores the total number of applicable months. This value is then multiplied by the results of 20 / 12.
  2. Then we multiply this result by 2. The 2 here is selected because it will help us with rounding the decimals later on, as there are 2 x 0,5 within 1, and we want decimals to be either 0, or 0,5.
  3. Afterwards we round the decimals, and lastly we divide the value by 2 again, as we already multiplied them.

The result of these operations can be found below:

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,673,3356,678,331011,6713,331516,6718,3320
x 2 3,336,661013,3316,662023,3326,663033,3336,6640
Round3710131720232730333740
/ 21,53,556,58,51011,513,51516,518,520

As seen, we now have the desired results, and job's done!

If you have different rounding criterions, you can easily adjust the number 2 we used above, and change this value according to your requirements. As an example, if you want to round the accruals to the closest 0,2 fractions, then you can use number 5 while multiplying and dividing.

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,673,3356,678,331011,6713,331516,6718,3320
x 58,3316,662533,3341,665058,3366,667583,3391,66100
Round817253342505867758392100
/ 51,63,456,68,41011,613,41516,618,420

 

Summary

I always have fun during adjusting the business rules and brainstorm about such calculations. I hope that this article will be helpful for you during your projects! Please also check the other blogs related to this topic.

Also please feel free to give feedback or comment.

Best,
Altug

entry.title: Re: Activate Draft instance on POST call
entry.content:
Hi @Shahab2 

If you want to create entries directly in the persistence table without passing through the Draft table, then you don't need to use the ACTIVATE action or so.

When you call the MODIFY ENTITIES CREATE sentence set:

%is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-off
